feed.title: Pronounce — recent dictionary additions
feed.subtitle: How engineers actually pronounce project / product / jargon names. Community-maintained.

entry.title: bahdanau — bah dah now
entry.summary: bahdanau is pronounced "bah dah now" (/ˌbɑːdɑːˈnaʊ/). Dzmitry Bahdanau, co-author of neural attention.

entry.title: brockman — brock mun
entry.summary: brockman is pronounced "brock mun" (/ˈbrɒkmən/). Greg Brockman, co-founder & president of OpenAI.

entry.title: chollet — show lay
entry.summary: chollet is pronounced "show lay" (/ʃɔːˈleɪ/). François Chollet, creator of Keras; "show-LAY". French.

entry.title: devlin — dev lin
entry.summary: devlin is pronounced "dev lin" (/ˈdɛvlɪn/). Jacob Devlin, lead author of BERT.

entry.title: goodfellow — good fellow
entry.summary: goodfellow is pronounced "good fellow" (/ˈɡʊdfɛloʊ/). Ian Goodfellow, inventor of GANs.

entry.title: hochreiter — hoke ry ter
entry.summary: hochreiter is pronounced "hoke ry ter" (/ˈhɔːxraɪtər/). Sepp Hochreiter, co-inventor of LSTM. German.

entry.title: howard — how erd
entry.summary: howard is pronounced "how erd" (/ˈhaʊərd/). Jeremy Howard, co-founder of fast.ai.

entry.title: huang — hwang
entry.summary: huang is pronounced "hwang" (/hwɑːŋ/). Jensen Huang, co-founder & CEO of NVIDIA; "hwang".

entry.title: kingma — king muh
entry.summary: kingma is pronounced "king muh" (/ˈkɪŋmə/). Diederik Kingma, co-author of Adam and the VAE.

entry.title: krizhevsky — krih zhef skee
entry.summary: krizhevsky is pronounced "krih zhef skee" (/krɪˈʒɛfski/). Alex Krizhevsky, lead author of AlexNet.

entry.title: mikolov — mee koh lof
entry.summary: mikolov is pronounced "mee koh lof" (/ˈmiːkəlɒf/). Tomáš Mikolov, creator of word2vec. Czech.

entry.title: mnih — muh nee
entry.summary: mnih is pronounced "muh nee" (/məˈniː/). Volodymyr Mnih, lead author of the DQN / Atari paper.

entry.title: ng — ing
entry.summary: ng is pronounced "ing" (/ɪŋ/). Andrew Ng (Coursera, deeplearning.ai); roughly "ng/ing", often spelled "N-G".

entry.title: polosukhin — po lo soo kin
entry.summary: polosukhin is pronounced "po lo soo kin" (/ˌpɒloʊˈsuːkɪn/). Illia Polosukhin, co-author of "Attention Is All You Need".

entry.title: raffel — raf ul
entry.summary: raffel is pronounced "raf ul" (/ˈræfəl/). Colin Raffel, lead author of T5.

entry.title: ramesh — rah mesh
entry.summary: ramesh is pronounced "rah mesh" (/ˈrɑːmɛʃ/). Aditya Ramesh, creator of DALL·E.

entry.title: rombach — rom bahk
entry.summary: rombach is pronounced "rom bahk" (/ˈrɒmbɑːx/). Robin Rombach, lead author of Stable Diffusion / latent diffusion. German.

entry.title: schmidhuber — shmit hoo ber
entry.summary: schmidhuber is pronounced "shmit hoo ber" (/ˈʃmɪthuːbər/). Jürgen Schmidhuber, co-inventor of LSTM. German.

entry.title: shazeer — shuh zeer
entry.summary: shazeer is pronounced "shuh zeer" (/ʃəˈzɪər/). Noam ("NOH-um") Shazeer, Transformer co-author, founder of Character.AI.

entry.title: szegedy — seg eh dee
entry.summary: szegedy is pronounced "seg eh dee" (/ˈsɛɡɛdi/). Christian Szegedy (Inception, adversarial examples). Hungarian.

entry.title: uszkoreit — oosh kor ite
entry.summary: uszkoreit is pronounced "oosh kor ite" (/ˈuːʃkɔːraɪt/). Jakob Uszkoreit, co-author of the Transformer. German.

entry.title: vaswani — vuh swah nee
entry.summary: vaswani is pronounced "vuh swah nee" (/vəˈswɑːni/). Ashish Vaswani, lead author of "Attention Is All You Need".

entry.title: vinyals — vin yals
entry.summary: vinyals is pronounced "vin yals" (/viˈɲals/). Oriol ("oh-ree-OL") Vinyals, DeepMind (Seq2Seq, AlphaStar). Catalan.

entry.title: wojciech — voy tekh
entry.summary: wojciech is pronounced "voy tekh" (/ˈvɔɪtɛx/). Polish first name (e.g. Wojciech Zaremba, OpenAI); "VOY-tekh".

entry.title: zaremba — zuh rem buh
entry.summary: zaremba is pronounced "zuh rem buh" (/zəˈrɛmbə/). Wojciech Zaremba, co-founder of OpenAI. Polish.
